case_id: 8 TIFF images with varying focus levels (synthetic cells)

pi_items.source: task.json description states 'You must measure at least 6 of the 8 images' and verifier.py line 65 checks for row_count >= 6
task.json description line 5: 'Sort the rows by StdDev in DESCENDING order' and verifier.py checks for stddev sorting
task.json description: 'DESCENDING order (highest StdDev = sharpest focus first)' and verifier.py line 126 checks is_sorted_desc

pi_items.verified_value: BBBC005 (Broad Bioimage Benchmark Collection) - Synthetic cells dataset from Broad Institute
pi_items.source: Web search and direct verification: BBBC005 is a real dataset from https://bbbc.broadinstitute.org/BBBC005. Setup script attempts to download from https://data.broadinstitute.org/bbbc/BBBC005/BBBC005_v1_images.zip (verified accessible, HTTP 200, 1.8GB). BBBC005 contains 19,200 simulated fluorescent cell images at varying focus levels using Gaussian blur (blur 1-48) for validation of focus metrics.

privileged_info_summary: The focus_quality_ranking task uses BBBC005, a verified real dataset from the Broad Bioimage Benchmark Collection containing synthetic cell images at varying focus levels. The task requires agents to measure pixel intensity statistics (Mean, StdDev, Min, Max) on at least 6 of 8 TIFF images and output results as a CSV file sorted by StdDev in descending order (highest StdDev = sharpest focus). Standard deviation serves as a proxy for image focus sharpness. The output file path is /home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results/focus_quality_report.csv and input images are located at /home/ga/ImageJ_Data/focus_assessment/. BBBC005 images are simulated (not real microscopy) and generated using SIMCEP platform with Gaussian blur simulation.
